header.subtitle: Module 3: Advanced Planning & Governance
sec.maturity.intro: The more mature your documentation is, the less the AI needs to guess. Part 1 covered the minimum viable setup. Here we move beyond the basics into corporate governance. The chart shows how specific documents eliminate development risks.
sec.maturity.card.1.title: Context Scalability
sec.maturity.card.1.desc: Granular documents allow the AI to load only what's needed for each task, saving tokens and maintaining accuracy.
sec.maturity.card.2.title: Decision Safety
sec.maturity.card.2.desc: With a decision history (ADRs), the AI stops suggesting patterns the team already decided to abandon in the past.
sec.maturity.card.3.title: The Natural Evolution
sec.maturity.card.3.desc:
Part 1: CLAUDE.md + TECH_STACK + ARCHITECTURE
Part 3: + Full Constitution + Plan.md ritual
sec.constitution.intro: An advanced documentation structure is what we call a "Constitution": it defines the non-negotiable boundaries for the AI in the project. Each category serves a distinct purpose in the model's mind.
sec.constitution.card.1.title: Behavior (LLM)
sec.constitution.card.1.desc: Defines who the AI is and how it should act.
sec.constitution.card.2.title: Design & Patterns
sec.constitution.card.2.desc: Prevents the AI from inventing unapproved structures or patterns.
sec.constitution.card.3.title: History & Decisions
sec.constitution.card.3.desc: Prevents the AI from repeating past mistakes or reverting decisions already made.
sec.constitution.tip.title: The Index Trick: LLM_CONTEXT.md
sec.constitution.tip.desc: With many documents, the AI may not know which one to read first. LLM_CONTEXT.md acts as a router: it instructs the AI to load the right document for each type of task.
sec.lifecycle.intro: The plan.md is where the AI "thinks out loud" before writing a single line of code. You approve the strategy before execution, eliminating rework from wrong architecture decisions.
sec.lifecycle.step.1.title: The Proposal
sec.lifecycle.step.1.desc: You ask: "Before you start, create a plan.md listing every file that will be changed and why." The AI maps the full impact of the change.
sec.lifecycle.step.2.title: The Architect's Review
sec.lifecycle.step.2.desc: You read the plan. If the AI wants to modify a Provider you don't want touched, or ignores an edge case, you correct the plan before execution, not after.
sec.lifecycle.step.3.title: Low-Friction Execution
sec.lifecycle.step.3.desc: With the strategy approved, the AI executes. Logic and architecture errors drop drastically, because they were eliminated in step 2.
sec.lifecycle.example.label: 📄 plan.md | Real example
sec.decision.intro: Unclogging the bottleneck means stop looking at semicolons and start looking at architecture. The Plan.md ritual changes the type of conversation you have with the AI.
sec.decision.wrong.title: The Old Bottleneck
sec.decision.wrong.li.2: AI decides the architecture, dev only finds out in the code.
sec.decision.wrong.li.3: Rework from wrong decisions already coded.
sec.decision.right.title: The New Flow
sec.decision.right.li.2: You approve the strategy in Plan.md before the code exists.
sec.decision.right.li.3: Architectural rework is eliminated by design.
sec.decision.prompts.title: High-Value Strategy Prompts
sec.decision.prompts.risk.label: Risk Prompt
sec.decision.prompts.risk.text: "Before applying this plan, analyze the RISKS.md and list 3 possible side effects of this change in the authentication layer."
sec.decision.prompts.history.label: Historical Verification Prompt
sec.decision.prompts.history.text: "Before creating a new cache layer, check DECISIONS.md. Have we already made a decision about caching that I should know about?"
sec.decision.prompts.scope.label: Scope Prompt
sec.decision.prompts.scope.text: "In the plan.md, explicitly list the files that should NOT be modified in this task."
sec.decision.mantra.label: The Part 3 Mantra:
sec.decision.mantra.text: "We approve strategies, not lines of code. The AI executes what the architect approved."
sec.limits.intro: Excessive governance is as harmful as insufficient governance. Too much documentation or poorly maintained docs can confuse the AI more than help it.
sec.limits.risks.title: Risks to avoid
sec.limits.risk.1.title: Documentation Rot
sec.limits.risk.1.desc: Documents that are not updated become outdated and start lying to the AI. A TECH_STACK.md with React 17 when you use 19 is worse than not having the file at all.
sec.limits.risk.1.tip: ✓ Assign an owner per document and review at the start of each sprint.
sec.limits.risk.2.title: Too Much Documentation
sec.limits.risk.2.desc: 30 fragmented context files consume the AI's context before it even starts working. If the AI needs to read 15 files to understand a simple task, the structure is wrong.
sec.limits.risk.2.tip: ✓ Use LLM_CONTEXT.md as an index: only load what's needed for each task.
sec.limits.attention.title: Points of attention
sec.limits.attention.1.title: Conflicting Rules
sec.limits.attention.1.desc: If CLAUDE.md says "use Zustand" and CODING_GUIDELINES.md says "avoid global state", the AI will oscillate between the two. Silent conflicts are hard to diagnose.
sec.limits.attention.1.tip: ✓ When changing a rule, search for contradictions in the other files.
sec.limits.attention.2.title: Plan.md is not an immutable contract
sec.limits.attention.2.desc: During execution, the AI may discover the plan was infeasible for a technical reason. This is normal. The AI should notify you and propose a revision, not execute anyway or stop silently. Instruct this explicitly in CLAUDE.md.
sec.limits.attention.3.title: Governance does not eliminate human review
sec.limits.attention.3.desc: Advanced documentation reduces pattern errors, not business logic errors. The AI can generate perfectly structured code that solves the wrong problem. Reviewing the intent of the solution is still a human responsibility.
sec.limits.practices.title: Best practices
sec.limits.practices.li.1: Start with 3-5 files. Only add a new one when you feel the need for it.
sec.limits.practices.li.2: Review the Constitution every time a rule is violated: update the docs, don't just correct it in the chat.
sec.limits.practices.li.3: Add an update date in the header of each context file.
sec.limits.practices.li.4: Version all context files alongside the code. PRs should include doc changes.
sec.nextsteps.intro: Evolve from what was created in Part 1. Four steps to go from minimum viable to a functional Constitution.
sec.nextsteps.step.1.title: Create DECISIONS.md with the team's last 3 decisions
sec.nextsteps.step.1.desc: It doesn't need to be retroactive. Document the 3 most recent decisions the AI should know before suggesting anything.
sec.nextsteps.step.2.title: Turn CLAUDE.md into a routing index
sec.nextsteps.step.2.desc: Add a section that instructs the AI to load specific documents for each type of task.
sec.nextsteps.step.3.title: Run the Plan.md ritual on a real task
sec.nextsteps.step.3.desc: Take the next feature from your backlog and run the full ritual: request the plan, review, approve, execute.
sec.nextsteps.step.3.prompt: "Read the CLAUDE.md and REQUIREMENTS.md. Before writing any code, create a plan.md with the files that will be modified and the risks you identified."
sec.nextsteps.step.4.title: Schedule a quarterly Constitution review
sec.nextsteps.step.4.desc: Rotted documentation is worse than absent documentation. Set aside 30 minutes per quarter to review each context file with the team.
sec.nextsteps.step.4.checklist: Quarterly checklist: are versions in TECH_STACK still correct? Are decisions in DECISIONS.md still valid? Are rules in CLAUDE.md still followed?
